siteSessionTitle: Redis Deep Dive — Architecture & Data Types
siteSessionFocus: Redis internals, strings, hashes, sorted sets

scenes.narration: Redis sirf key-value store nahi hai — sorted sets se leaderboards, streams se event queues. Yeh internals FAANG mein advanced question hai.

Redis single-threaded event loop architecture — IO multiplexing se concurrency handle karta hai, no mutex overhead. Data types: Strings (get/set/incr/expire), Hashes (HSET/HGETALL — user profiles), Lists (LPUSH/RPOP — queues), Sets (SADD/SMEMBERS — unique visitors), Sorted Sets (ZADD/ZRANGE — leaderboards, rate limiting), Streams (XADD/XREAD — event sourcing). Persistence: RDB snapshots (point-in-time), AOF (append-only log, durability). Redis 7.0: multi-threading for IO but command processing still single-threaded.

Redis single-thread bottleneck: KEYS * command production pe mat use karna — O(N) hai aur poora Redis block karta hai. Alternative: SCAN command iterates in chunks — non-blocking. Big key problem: ek Redis key mein 10MB data — serialization aur network transfer slow hoga. Solution: key ko smaller chunks mein split karo. LRANGE kisi bhi size list pe — O(S+N) hai, large lists pe slow. Yeh operational gotchas FAANG SRE interviews mein aksar poochhe jaate hain.

Redis sirf cache nahi — session store, queue, leaderboard, pub-sub, distributed lock sab kuch hai. Right data structure choose karna performance 10x impact karta hai. Guru-sishya.in pe Redis data types ke practice problems karo.
